title: rubus caesius var. rivalis
intro: Rubus caesius var. rivalis, also known as the black thorn or wild raspberry, is a variety of the blackthorn plant that belongs to the Rosaceae family. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine, where its medicinal properties include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus caesius var. rivalis are attributed to its ability to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and soothe skin irritations, making it a popular choice for natural remedies. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat various health conditions, from wound healing to respiratory problems.
